channels.description: Game industry talks, production lessons, design methods and business strategy from GDC.

channels.name: Game Maker's Toolkit

channels.name: Noclip
channels.description: Studio documentaries, developer interviews and production deep dives.

videos.title: The Making of Disco Elysium - Part Four: Art
videos.zhTitle: The Making of Disco Elysium - Part Four: Art
videos.description: In the forth episode of our series on the development of Disco Elysium we talk to some of the art team about the process of crafting the visual language of this iconic game.

videos.title: The Making of Disco Elysium - Part Three: Writing
videos.zhTitle: The Making of Disco Elysium - Part Three: Writing
videos.description: In the third episode of our Disco Elysium series we explore how the writing team tackled writing the games characters and building out the narrative lore of Elysium.

videos.title: The Making of Disco Elysium - Part Two: Building Elysium
videos.zhTitle: The Making of Disco Elysium - Part Two: Building Elysium
videos.description: In the second episode in our multi-part series on the development of Disco Elysium, we reveal how the team built a makeshift studio around themselves in central Tallinn, and began the work of building Elysium.

videos.title: The World Design of Hollow Knight: Silksong
videos.zhTitle: The World Design of Hollow Knight: Silksong
videos.description: Team Cherry returns to the world of Hollow Knight with Silksong - a new game, with a new character, in a whole new world. In this episode of Boss Keys let's look at how the developers designed Pharloom and the Citadel, and how they thought about topics like progression, backtracking, and optional content.

videos.title: The Making of Disco Elysium - Part One: Foundations
videos.zhTitle: The Making of Disco Elysium - Part One: Foundations
videos.description: In the first of our multi-part series on Disco Elysium we travel to Tallinn, Estonia to explore how a group of post-soviet high school dropouts were inspired to create a video game.

videos.title: What's the Point of Prototyping?
videos.zhTitle: What's the Point of Prototyping?
videos.description: With a game idea under your belt, it's time to test it out. And the best way to do that is with a prototype. Find out why, and get tips for making your own, in episode two of Game Dev 101.

videos.title: The Making of Indiana Jones and the Great Circle - /noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: The Making of Indiana Jones and the Great Circle - /noclip Documentary
videos.description: In the third and final video from our time at MachineGames we dive into the design and development of Indiana Jones and the Great Circle.

videos.title: How Games Do Destruction
videos.zhTitle: How Games Do Destruction
videos.description: Let's talk about video game destruction, and break down the techniques used to dent cars, break boxes, tear through scenery, and blow up buildings.

videos.title: What's The Point Of Hard Games, Anyway?
videos.zhTitle: What's The Point Of Hard Games, Anyway?
videos.description: Hollow Knight: Silksong is the latest game to join the "difficulty in games" debate. In this Mini episode of GMTK, let's look at why, and how, designers implement a high level of challenge in their games.

videos.title: The Making of Wolfenstein's New Order and New Colossus - /noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: The Making of Wolfenstein's New Order and New Colossus - /noclip Documentary
videos.description: We revisit the team at MachineGames to explore the development of their Wolfenstein series.

videos.title: The Origins of MachineGames (Riddick, The Darkness) - Noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: The Origins of MachineGames (Riddick, The Darkness) - Noclip Documentary
videos.description: We chronicle the pre-history of Machine Games by looking into the early days of Starbreeze Studios, and how the studio came to work on the Wolfenstein franchise.

videos.title: My Favourite Games from GMTK Game Jam 2025
videos.zhTitle: My Favourite Games from GMTK Game Jam 2025
videos.description: This year's GMTK Game Jam was all about loops. Here are 20 of my favourite entries to the jam - from programming chefs to time-travelling crows.

videos.title: Marketing Your Game Under the New Steam Rules
videos.zhTitle: Marketing Your Game Under the New Steam Rules
videos.description: 2024 was the year Valve made major changes on how games and their demos gain visibility. In this 2025 Independent Games Summit talk, game marketing researcher Chris Zukowski lists the changes and show how successful developers have adapted to them.

videos.title: How Games Are Made: TREES
videos.zhTitle: How Games Are Made: TREES
videos.description: Produced by Danny O'Dwyer & Jeremy Jayne

videos.title: The Story Behind Battlefield 3's Divisive Campaign - Noclip
videos.zhTitle: The Story Behind Battlefield 3's Divisive Campaign - Noclip
videos.description: Danny chats to David Goldfarb (Lead Designer - Single Player) about the process of creating the divisive Battlefield 3 campaign.

videos.zhTitle: How I Made Word Play

videos.title: Building Big Impact, One Brick at a Time: Marginal Gains in 'LEGO Horizon Adventures'
videos.zhTitle: Building Big Impact, One Brick at a Time: Marginal Gains in 'LEGO Horizon Adventures'
videos.description: If you break down everything that goes into making a game, and improve each thing by just one percent, you'll have a significant improvement when you put them all together. This GDC 2025 talk from Studio Gobo's Lead Designer Adam Russell goes into detail about how to build marginal gains into your team's development process, and create a mindset that empowers individual team members to identify and action micro-impro

videos.title: How Games Are Made: SOUND DESIGN
videos.zhTitle: How Games Are Made: SOUND DESIGN
videos.description: Produced by Danny O'Dwyer & Jeremy Jayne

videos.title: How I got my demo ready for Steam Next Fest
videos.zhTitle: How I got my demo ready for Steam Next Fest
videos.description: It's Steam Next Fest, and I've got a game in the showcase. So here are 7 tips for making the most of this demo sharing festival.

videos.title: Evolving Worlds from Chaos: The Art-Led Approach of 'Darkest Dungeon 2's' Procedural Generation
videos.zhTitle: Evolving Worlds from Chaos: The Art-Led Approach of 'Darkest Dungeon 2's' Procedural Generation
videos.description: In an industry where procedural generation systems are historically programmer-led, Red Hook Studios took an unconventional approach and crafted a system that prioritized the game's art direction instead. In this GDC 2025 session, Marielle Fox, artist, and Colin Towle, programmer,  discuss what it took to create the system that generates Darkest Dungeon 2's crumbling, post-apocalyptic world.

videos.title: Rules of the Game 2025: Uncommon Techniques from Insightful Designers
videos.zhTitle: Rules of the Game 2025: Uncommon Techniques from Insightful Designers
videos.description: Every designer knows that game rules are what create gameplay, but figuring out the rules we use to make games is entirely more difficult. In addition, game design rules are much more open to personal style and preference. The popular Rules of the Game session is a great place to see just how wide-ranging design opinions can be. Returning for 2025, this session brings together a cohort of experienced game designers,

videos.title: Rules of the Game: Five Tricks of Highly Effective Designers
videos.zhTitle: Rules of the Game: Five Tricks of Highly Effective Designers
videos.description: Every working designer has them: unique techniques or "tricks" that they use when crafting gameplay. Sure, there's the general game design wisdom that everyone agrees on and can be found in many a game design book, but experienced game designers often have very specific rules that are personal to them, techniques that not everyone knows about or even agrees with. In this GDC 2015 session, five experienced game design

videos.title: Blue Prince: Can a random puzzle game actually work?
videos.zhTitle: Blue Prince: Can a random puzzle game actually work?
videos.description: Blue Prince is a curious mix of genres: part adventure game, part deck-building roguelike. But do those two ingredients mix?

videos.title: Rendering 'Assassin's Creed Shadows'
videos.zhTitle: Rendering 'Assassin's Creed Shadows'
videos.description: Assassin's Creed Shadows is a large systemic open world game with dynamic time of day cycles, a systemic weather system, and seasons. Such a large world and dynamic setting poses many rendering challenges. This GDC 2025 talk focuses on a typical frame and walks the audience through the various steps required to produce the final render, focusing on gpu driven pipeline, lighting improvements and other new developments

videos.title: Designing The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion - (From Our 2018 Interviews)
videos.zhTitle: Designing The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion - (From Our 2018 Interviews)
videos.description: To celebrate the launch of Oblivion Remastered we re-edited all of the Oblivion-related clips (and added some new ones) from our 2018 Bethesda interviews into a single video. Links to the full videos used are below.

videos.title: Life After the Success of Dwarf Fortress - (Series Episode Four)
videos.zhTitle: Life After the Success of Dwarf Fortress - (Series Episode Four)
videos.description: We close out our four-part series on Dwarf Fortress with an focused on everything that happened after the Steam launch. From the addition of Adventure Mode to some big life changes for Tarn, Zach and Annie.

videos.title: How Dwarf Fortress Coming to Steam Changed Everything - (Series Episode 3)
videos.zhTitle: How Dwarf Fortress Coming to Steam Changed Everything - (Series Episode 3)
videos.description: In the third episode in our Dwarf Fortress Series we cover the development of the Steam version of Dwarf Fortress and how the brothers lives changed after launch.

videos.title: How Dwarf Fortress Evolved over 16 Years of Development - (Series Episode Two)
videos.zhTitle: How Dwarf Fortress Evolved over 16 Years of Development - (Series Episode Two)
videos.description: In the second video in our series on Dwarf Fortress we expore the ASCII era of the game and how Tarn and Zach kept the lights on while updating a free game for almost two decades.

videos.title: The Origins of Dwarf Fortress - (Series Episode One)
videos.zhTitle: The Origins of Dwarf Fortress - (Series Episode One)
videos.description: In the first episode of this four-part series we explore the  decisions that led a pair of brothers to embark on the development journey of a lifetime.

videos.title: The Past, Present & Future of Soul Reaver - Noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: The Past, Present & Future of Soul Reaver - Noclip Documentary
videos.description: What does it take to bring back a franchise? We talk to people from around the Soul Reaver universe to try and explain its initial appeal, enduring legacy and possible futures.

videos.title: The Origin Story Behind Counter-Strike's Most Iconic Map - Noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: The Origin Story Behind Counter-Strike's Most Iconic Map - Noclip Documentary
videos.description: Jeremy Jayne reveals the remarkable tale of how a teenage hobbyist (Dave Johnston) accidentally made one of Counter-Strike's most iconic maps. And then accidentally did it again.

videos.title: The Design of Dredge - Noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: The Design of Dredge - Noclip Documentary
videos.description: We sit down with Black Salt Games to talk about the design of their cosmic horror fishing game DREDGE.

videos.title: Wipeout 2097: The Making of an Iconic PlayStation Soundtrack - Noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: Wipeout 2097: The Making of an Iconic PlayStation Soundtrack - Noclip Documentary
videos.description: We explore the creation of one of gaming's most iconic soundtracks by talking to Psygnosis composer Tim "CoLD SToRAGE" Wright about a very special era of 90s games development.

videos.title: Embracing Chaos - How Hyper Light Breaker Survived 2023
videos.zhTitle: Embracing Chaos - How Hyper Light Breaker Survived 2023
videos.description: With Embracer Group engaged in a massive sell-off, the team at Heart Machine try to focus on the road to early access, unsure about what the future holds for their publisher.

videos.title: Saving Video Game History - Here's What We Found (The First 100 Videos)
videos.zhTitle: Saving Video Game History - Here's What We Found (The First 100 Videos)
videos.description: Let's talk about the first 100 videos on our archive channel.

videos.title: The Making of Pentiment - Noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: The Making of Pentiment - Noclip Documentary
videos.description: We travel to Obsidian Entertainment to talk to the small team behind this award winning historical role-playing game.

videos.title: Why Monolith Mattered
videos.zhTitle: Why Monolith Mattered
videos.description: WB Games just shut down the long-running, storied, and successful game developer Monolith Productions. Let's remember this studio by looking back at my five favourite games.

videos.title: How to find amazing game ideas
videos.zhTitle: How to find amazing game ideas
videos.description: The first step to making your next game is to come up with an idea. But where do game designers get their ideas? And how do you know if the idea is worth pursuing? Let's find out, in part one of Game Dev 101.

videos.title: Can I fix Zelda's UI using Unity?
videos.zhTitle: Can I fix Zelda's UI using Unity?
videos.description: The Legend of Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom has some baffling user interface design. So I decided to make some alternative versions in Unity to see if we could improve things.

videos.title: A detective demo inspired by Geoguessr
videos.zhTitle: A detective demo inspired by Geoguessr
videos.description: Weekender is a series where I recommend interesting new and upcoming indie games that GMTK fans should check out.

videos.title: How a Super Smash Bros. Fighter is Designed
videos.zhTitle: How a Super Smash Bros. Fighter is Designed
videos.description: Super Smash Bros. features 89 different characters from across gaming history. How does director Masahiro Sakurai keep them cohesive and balanced while honouring their individual legacies? Let's find out.

videos.title: What it's like to release a game on Steam
videos.zhTitle: What it's like to release a game on Steam
videos.description: Developing was an on-going YouTube series, where I shared the step-by-step process of making my first video game: Mind Over Magnet!

videos.title: I made a game about magnets (Developing 16)
videos.zhTitle: I made a game about magnets (Developing 16)
videos.description: Developing is an on-going YouTube series, where I share the step-by-step process of making my first video game: Mind Over Magnet!

videos.title: 10 Game Design Lessons from 10 Years of GMTK
videos.zhTitle: 10 Game Design Lessons from 10 Years of GMTK
videos.description: Game Maker's Toolkit just turned 10 years old! Here are the ten biggest lessons I've learned from the last decade of learning about game design.

videos.title: What Makes a Game Feel Mysterious?
videos.zhTitle: What Makes a Game Feel Mysterious?
videos.description: What makes a game feel mysterious? And how do we make a player feel like an active participant in solving the mystery? Let's dig in to some amazing games.

videos.title: The Best Games from GMTK Game Jam 2024
videos.zhTitle: The Best Games from GMTK Game Jam 2024
videos.description: The GMTK Game Jam for 2024 was enormous. In this video, Mark runs down his 20 favourite games, from a jam where every game fit the theme “Built to Scale".

videos.title: An excellent block-pushing puzzler on an open ocean
videos.zhTitle: An excellent block-pushing puzzler on an open ocean
videos.description: Isles of Sea and Sky is a brilliant box-pushing puzzle game, with a potent open-world design. It's the next entry in a series of indie game recommendations called "Weekender".

videos.title: The 100 Games That Taught Me Game Design
videos.zhTitle: The 100 Games That Taught Me Game Design
videos.description: One of the best ways to learn about game design is to just play a whole bunch of games. But with thousands of titles to choose from... where do you start? Well, this video lists 100 games that have most helped me in my journey to understand game development - from arcade classics to virtual reality thrills.

videos.title: Balatro's 'Cursed' Design Problem
videos.zhTitle: Balatro's 'Cursed' Design Problem
videos.description: Balatro is the current game design darling. But even the designer is aware of a ‘fundamental design flaw’. What is it, can it be fixed, and what does it teach us about making games for people?

videos.title: I just changed my entire game (Developing 13)
videos.zhTitle: I just changed my entire game (Developing 13)
videos.description: Developing is an on-going YouTube series, where I share the step-by-step process of making my first video game: Mind Over Magnet!

videos.title: Pondering Orbs: The Rendering and Art Tools of 'COCOON'
videos.zhTitle: Pondering Orbs: The Rendering and Art Tools of 'COCOON'
videos.description: This talk details the graphical techniques developed for COCOON, the debut title of Geometric Interactive, founded by Jeppe Carlsen, lead gameplay designer on LIMBO and INSIDE.

videos.title: How to Launch an Online Game and Still Attend the Launch Party
videos.zhTitle: How to Launch an Online Game and Still Attend the Launch Party
videos.description: Launch day for an online game is a very exciting time, but often not in a good way for the online or service engineering team. Odyssey Interactive's debut title, Omega Strikers, a competitive online sports-combat game launched on 7 platforms with a services team of 3 reaching hundreds of thousands of players on day 1 with no outages. This GDC 2024 session explores the core role that Observability played in making thi

videos.title: Returning to Monkey Island - Noclip Documentary
videos.zhTitle: Returning to Monkey Island - Noclip Documentary
videos.description: We talk to the original creators of Monkey Island about the legacy of the franchise and how that impacted their journey to bring the series back in 2023.

videos.title: Life After 76: Why this Veteran Bethesda World Designer Went Solo
videos.zhTitle: Life After 76: Why this Veteran Bethesda World Designer Went Solo
videos.description: We talk to Nate Purkeypile about the reception to Fallout 76, why he left Bethesda after 14 years of crafting worlds there, and what it's like making a solo open world game "The Axis Unseen" using Unreal.
